question: Does the image clearly depict a mechanical watch movement exploded into its components (e.g., gears, rotor, case parts)?
Is the number '3120' visible on the rotor component in a simplified but recognizable form?
Are the blued screws from the original movement represented with minimal blue color accents?
Is the image rendered as a clean, flat vector graphic, completely free of photorealistic textures, noise, or shading gradients?
Does the color palette consist primarily of black, white, and gray, with limited blocks of primary colors (red, blue, yellow) consistent with the Bauhaus reference?
Are the mechanical components abstracted into simplified geometric shapes (circles, arcs, rectangles) rather than realistic metallic textures?
Does the image strictly follow the vertical isometric exploded layout, with components stacked along a central vertical axis?
Is the stacking order consistent with the structure reference (Top: Bezel/Crystal -> Dial -> Movement -> Rotor -> Case -> Bottom: Caseback)?
Are annotation boxes with leader lines present on the right side, matching the spatial arrangement of the structure reference?
Are the annotation boxes free of readable text (containing only empty space or abstract lines)?
Are the edges of the vector shapes sharp and anti-aliased, without jagged boundaries or compression artifacts?
Is the image free from logical geometric distortions (e.g., melted rings, warped ellipses) and random visual noise?
